metadata.name: Mulldrifter evoke — ETB draw 2 resolves inline, then evoke sacrifice trigger
metadata.description: P1 casts Mulldrifter for its evoke cost ({2}{U}). Mulldrifter enters the battlefield. Its ETB 'draw 2 cards' effect fires inline during resolution (via fire_when_enters_triggered_effects, not via the stack). The evoke sacrifice trigger (CR 702.74a) then goes on the stack as a separate trigger. Both players pass priority; the evoke sacrifice trigger resolves and Mulldrifter is sacrificed to P1's graveyard. Net result: Mulldrifter is in the graveyard, P1 drew 2 cards — the classic 'sorcery that draws 2 for {2}{U}' effect.
metadata.generation_notes: Mulldrifter oracle: '2/2 Elemental — Flying. When Mulldrifter enters, draw 2 cards. Evoke {2}{U}.' CR 702.74a: Evoke represents two abilities: (1) a static alternative-cost ability ('may cast for evoke cost instead of mana cost') and (2) a triggered ability ('when this permanent enters, if evoke cost was paid, sacrifice it'). Engine implementation detail: Mulldrifter's ETB draw-2 ability is executed inline during resolution via fire_when_enters_triggered_effects (resolution.rs:290-297), which runs WhenEntersBattlefield effects from the card definition immediately at resolution time — NOT as a separate stackable trigger. This means P1 draws 2 cards before the evoke sacrifice trigger is placed on the stack. The evoke sacrifice trigger is collected by check_triggers from the PermanentEnteredBattlefield event (abilities.rs:573-588) and placed on the stack as a EvokeSacrificeTrigger. Result: after Mulldrifter resolves, the stack has 1 item (evoke sacrifice trigger), P1 already has 2 cards in hand. After the evoke trigger resolves, Mulldrifter is in the graveyard and the stack is empty. Initial state: P1 has Mulldrifter in hand, {2}{U} in pool (colorless:2 + blue:1), and 2 cards in library for the draw-2 effect. The harness action 'cast_spell_evoke' sets cast_with_evoke: true so the engine uses the evoke cost ({2}{U}) instead of the normal cost ({4}{U}).

script.step_note: P1 casts Mulldrifter for its evoke cost; ETB draw fires inline, then evoke sacrifice trigger goes on stack

actions.description: Initial: Mulldrifter in P1 hand, battlefield empty, stack empty

actions.note: CR 702.74a + CR 601.2: P1 casts Mulldrifter for its evoke cost {2}{U} (alternative cost). cast_spell_evoke sets cast_with_evoke: true so the engine pays the evoke cost instead of the normal {4}{U}.

actions.description: After casting: Mulldrifter on stack, P1 hand empty
actions.note: Mulldrifter is on the stack as an evoke spell.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Mulldrifter resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Mulldrifter resolves and enters the battlefield under P1's control.

CR 603.2: ETB draw-2 effect fires inline during resolution (fire_when_enters_triggered_effects). P1 draws 2 cards immediately. This is not a separate stackable trigger in the current engine implementation.
CR 702.74a: Evoke sacrifice trigger fires after ETB — 'When this permanent enters, if its evoke cost was paid, sacrifice it.' Goes onto the stack as EvokeSacrificeTrigger.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Mulldrifter enters battlefield. ETB draw-2 executes inline (P1 draws 2 cards). Evoke sacrifice trigger is placed on the stack (1 trigger total).

actions.description: After Mulldrifter resolves: evoke sacrifice trigger on stack, Mulldrifter on battlefield, P1 already drew 2 cards
actions.note: Stack has 1 item: the evoke sacrifice trigger. P1 has 2 cards in hand (Island and Forest drawn inline by ETB effect). Mulldrifter is still on battlefield until the sacrifice trigger resolves.
CR 116.3: Both players pass — evoke sacrifice trigger resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.17a: P1 sacrifices Mulldrifter — it moves from the battlefield to P1's graveyard.

actions.note: CR 702.74a + CR 608.2: Evoke sacrifice trigger resolves; Mulldrifter is sacrificed to P1's graveyard.

actions.description: Final: Mulldrifter in graveyard, P1 has 2 cards in hand, battlefield empty, stack empty
actions.note: Net result: Mulldrifter is in P1's graveyard; P1 has 2 cards in hand. This is the 'sorcery that draws 2 for {2}{U}' effect of evoke. CR 702.74a fulfilled: evoke cost was paid, creature entered, draw-2 fired, sacrifice trigger resolved.
